text: 噢，如果这就是你想要知道的，那倒不费什么事。就在前天，我从法勒伦进城，有位朋友追上来，要我重讲阿伽松家那场会饮。

translations.en-US: If this is what you want to know, it is no trouble. Just the other day, as I was walking in from Phalerum, a friend stopped me and asked me to retell that banquet at Agathon's house.

text: 我们当时都还小，那是阿伽松凭第一部悲剧得奖之后的庆典。事情并不新近，我也是后来从阿里司托得姆那里听来的。

translations.en-US: We were still young then. It was after Agathon won with his first tragedy. The event was long ago, and I only heard the full account later from Aristodemus.

text: 我在路上碰上苏格拉底，那天他刚洗过澡，脚上还穿着漂亮鞋子。他问我：尽管没请你，愿不愿意跟我去阿伽松家吃晚饭？

translations.en-US: I met Socrates on the road. He had just bathed and was wearing fine sandals. He asked: though you were not invited, would you come with me to dine at Agathon's?

text: 到这边来，苏格拉底，坐在我边上，好让我分享你在门廊里想到的智慧。

translations.en-US: Come sit by me, Socrates, so I may share the wisdom you discovered in the portico.

text: 爱神是最古老也最尊贵的神；对青年而言，没有比高尚的爱人和被爱者更大的福分。

translations.en-US: Love is the oldest and most honored god; for the young, no blessing is greater than a noble lover and beloved.

text: 并非所有的爱都相同：既然阿佛洛狄忒有天上的和凡俗的两种，爱也有高下之分，应当区分可赞美与不可赞美的爱。

translations.en-US: Not every love is the same: since Aphrodite has both heavenly and common forms, love too must be judged by higher and lower kinds.

text: 爱的力量不只在人心，也在身体、音乐、季节和天象里；凡是和谐与节制之处，都能看到健全的爱。

translations.en-US: Love works not only in the soul, but in the body, music, seasons, and stars; wherever harmony and moderation appear, healthy love is present.

text: 人原本是完整的圆体，后来被神分开；每个人终其一生都在寻找自己的另一半，这种追寻就是爱。

translations.en-US: Humans were once whole and round, then split apart by the gods; all our lives we search for our other half, and that search is love.

text: 爱神最年轻、最柔美、最细腻，住在花与灵魂之中；在他的引导下，诗与语言都变得优雅。

translations.en-US: Love is youngest, gentlest, and most delicate, dwelling among flowers and souls; under his guidance, poetry and speech become graceful.

text: 爱总是对某物的爱，且总是对自己所缺乏之物的爱；既然如此，爱并不已经拥有它所追求的美与善。

translations.en-US: Love is always love of something, and of what one lacks; therefore love does not already possess the beauty and goodness it seeks.

text: 爱是贫乏神与资源神之子，所以既不全然匮乏，也不真正富足；他介于无知与智慧之间，永远在追求。

translations.en-US: Love is the child of Poverty and Resource, so he is neither wholly poor nor truly rich; he stands between ignorance and wisdom, always in pursuit.

text: 真正的上升之路，是从爱一个身体，到爱一切美的身体，再到爱灵魂、制度与知识，最终凝视美本身。

translations.en-US: The true ascent begins with one beautiful body, then all beautiful bodies, then beautiful souls, laws, and knowledge, and finally the vision of Beauty itself.

text: 这就是狄奥提玛的教义：若要使生命值得活，我们应当在美中生育德性，并终生赞美爱的力量。

translations.en-US: This is Diotima's teaching: if life is to be worthy, we must give birth to virtue in beauty and praise the power of love throughout our lives.

text: 苏格拉底像会开启的西勒诺斯雕像：外表粗朴可笑，内部却藏着神性的形象和最严肃的智慧。

translations.en-US: Socrates is like an opened Silenus statue: rough and comic outside, yet inside are divine images and the gravest wisdom.

text: 我曾试图以亲近换取他的偏爱，但他对肉身诱惑毫不动摇；在战场与生活中，他都显示出惊人的节制与坚忍。

translations.en-US: I tried to win his favor through intimacy, but he was unmoved by bodily temptation; in war and in life he showed astonishing restraint and endurance.

text: 后来狂欢者闯入，宴席秩序尽失。天将亮时只剩苏格拉底、阿里斯托芬和阿伽松仍在辩论，随后众人相继睡去。

translations.en-US: Then revelers rushed in and the order of the banquet collapsed. Near dawn only Socrates, Aristophanes, and Agathon were still arguing before all finally slept.
